name: ExtensionAttachment_Size
title: R5: size additional types
description: R5: `Attachment.size` additional types (integer64) additional types from child elements (value)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `Attachment.size` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`Attachment.size` 0..1 `integer64`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `Attachment.size` 0..1 `integer64`
*  R4B: `Attachment.size` 0..1 `unsignedInt`
*  R4: `Attachment.size` 0..1 `unsignedInt`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `Attachment.size` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Attachment.size` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.
The mappings for `Attachment.size` do not cover the following types: integer64.
The mappings for `Attachment.size` do not cover the following types based on type expansion: value.

element.definition: R5: `Attachment.size` additional types (integer64) additional types from child elements (value)
element.comment:
Element `Attachment.size` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `Attachment.size` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.
The mappings for `Attachment.size` do not cover the following types: integer64.
The mappings for `Attachment.size` do not cover the following types based on type expansion: value.
The number of bytes is redundant if the data is provided as a base64binary, but is useful if the data is provided as a url reference.

element.short: Number of bytes of content (if url provided)
element.definition: The number of bytes of data that make up this attachment (before base64 encoding, if that is done).
element.comment: The number of bytes is redundant if the data is provided as a base64binary, but is useful if the data is provided as a url reference.
